Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 09:33 ET
Dispersion trade setup emerges as index puts clash with single-name calls
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index P/C at 1.61 versus single-name P/C at 0.72 signals institutional dispersion hedging into monthly OpEx.
  • 2. Negative gamma environment amplifies all moves with SPY dealers short $5.6B — technical breakouts gain momentum.
  • 3. Vol surface backwardation across 17 names suggests elevated event risk ahead of major earnings cascade.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge setup

The most striking divergence today is the P/C split telling two different stories: index options are put-heavy at 1.61 while single-name flow tilts call-heavy at 0.72, a classic dispersion hedge pattern ahead of monthly OpEx. This setup coincides with implied correlation at 0.09 — the lowest stock-specific regime in months — creating an ideal environment for long single-name vol, short index vol strategies. The massive QQQ April 17 582P block (232k contracts) and SPY May 22 535P (150k contracts) anchor the index protection, while NVDA call blocks total 160k contracts across April strikes. SPY's negative gamma position at $-5.6B means dealer hedging will amplify any directional moves, particularly dangerous given the technical backdrop. AMD's golden cross formation with RSI at 70 aligns perfectly with its vol surface showing backwardation — the 87k NVDA call block appears to be momentum chasing rather than contrarian positioning. Meanwhile, TSLA's death cross at RSI 37 explains the 102k call block as a potential technical bounce play, though the bearish regime composite argues against sustainability. Vol divergence flags reveal institutional confusion: META trades with vol 139% rich to its sector ETF yet shows a "vol high/skew flat" pattern, while EWZ displays the rare "rich vol/call heavy" combination. These cross-signals ahead of GS and JPM earnings Monday suggest positioning for sector rotation rather than broad directional bets.

Layer 1: P/C divergence 1.61 vs 0.72Layer 2: SPY GEX -$5.6BLayer 4: IC 0.09 stock-specific
NVDABULLISH
Golden cross momentum chase
NVDA's golden cross formation with RSI at 61 aligns with 160k total call blocks across April 13 and April 17 190C strikes. The vol surface shows backwardation, suggesting elevated near-term event risk despite the technical breakout. Millennium's Q4 reversal from puts to conviction calls with 93% position increase supports the momentum narrative.
L3: 160k call blocksL5: golden cross RSI 61L7: Millennium +93% Q4
Long NVDA Apr 190C vs short QQQ calls
TSLABULLISH
Death cross bounce play
The 102k TSLA April 17 680C block appears contrarian given the death cross formation and RSI at 37, potentially positioning for a technical bounce off oversold levels. Vol surface backwardation suggests elevated near-term risk, making this a high-risk momentum counter-trend play rather than fundamental positioning.
L3: 102k call blockL5: death cross RSI 37L4: backwardation
TSLA Apr 680C vs 650P risk reversal
METAVOL PLAY
Rich vol skew disconnect
META presents a puzzling vol divergence pattern: IV trading 139% rich to its sector ETF while displaying 'vol high/skew flat' characteristics. The death cross formation contradicts the elevated vol premium, suggesting either stale hedge demand or positioning for earnings-driven sector rotation. Point72's Q4 reduction conflicts with the vol richness.
L4: IV +139% vs sectorL4: vol high/skew flatL5: death cross
Sell META vol vs buy sector ETF vol

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
